summary: env-stream: import-time remote code execution via worker Module._compile fetching attacker-controlled JavaScript
details: The package impersonates dotenv-flow (homepage and internal branding point at kerimdzhanov/dotenv-flow, README mass-rewritten to substitute the name `env-stream`). On require of the main module, it spawns a Node worker with `eval:true`, then HTTP GETs https://realase-0626.vercel.app/api/v1 and posts the response body to the worker. The worker's message handler compiles the received string into a Module via `m._compile(s1, 'error.js')` and invokes `m.exports()`, executing attacker-controlled JavaScript inside the installer's process at library load time. Any process that imports env-stream — expecting a drop-in dotenv-flow replacement — runs whatever code the operator of realase-0626.vercel.app serves at that moment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
